messages.content: You are MiOS-Engineer. Cite files when stating facts.
Where do I add a new package to MiOS?

Edit `usr/share/mios/PACKAGES.md` (the SSOT). Add the package name inside the appropriate fenced block — e.g. ```packages-base, ```packages-desktop, ```packages-critical, ```packages-self-build. Each block is parsed by `automation/lib/packages.sh:get_packages` with regex `^```packages-${category}$/,/^```$`. The Containerfile pre-pipeline RUN installs `packages-base`; other categories are installed by the relevant numbered phase script via `install_packages`, `install_packages_strict`, or `install_packages_optional`. Critical packages are post-validated via `rpm -q` (`automation/build.sh:285-300`).
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What is the canonical kargs.d format in MiOS?

Flat top-level array, no section header, no delete sub-key:

```toml
kargs = ["slab_nomerge", "lockdown=integrity"]
match-architectures = ["x86_64"]
```

Files live under `usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/` (top entry `00-mios.toml`). They are processed lexicographically; earlier entries cannot be removed by later files in the same image — use runtime `bootc kargs --delete` for removal. `bootc container lint` (the final RUN of `Containerfile`, LAW 4) rejects any other format.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
Why is init_on_alloc=1 not set in MiOS even though SecureBlue recommends it?

`init_on_alloc=1`, `init_on_free=1`, and `page_alloc.shuffle=1` are explicitly disabled in `usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/00-mios.toml` — they cause CUDA memory-init failures on NVIDIA. To re-enable them on a CPU-only build, drop a higher-priority kargs.d file (e.g. `01-cpu-only.toml`) with the desired entries; lexicographic precedence will win. SecureBlue is the audit baseline (`SECURITY.md` cites it); MiOS documents every divergence inline.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I verify the running MiOS image is genuinely from CI?

```sh
cosign verify \
  --certificate-identity-regexp="https://github.com/mios-dev/mios" \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer="https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
  ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest
```

The `.github/workflows/mios-ci.yml` workflow keyless-signs every tag and `main` push. For SLSA provenance: `cosign verify-attestation --type slsaprovenance ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest`.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I build a Hyper-V VHDX locally?

On Linux: set `MIOS_USER_PASSWORD_HASH=$(openssl passwd -6 'yourpass')` (and optionally `MIOS_SSH_PUBKEY`), then `just vhdx`. The recipe `sed`-substitutes the hash and pubkey into a temp copy of `config/artifacts/vhdx.toml`, runs BIB with `--type vhd` (BIB still emits VPC format), then `qemu-img convert -f vpc -O vhdx` to the final `.vhdx` in `output/`. Attach to a Gen 2 VM with Secure Boot using the **Microsoft UEFI CA** template (not 'Microsoft Windows'). On Windows: `.\preflight.ps1 ; .\mios-build-local.ps1`.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What's the AI surface contract on MiOS?

All system agents target the OpenAI-compatible endpoint at `http://localhost:8080/v1`, served by the LocalAI Quadlet at `etc/containers/systemd/mios-ai.container` (LAW 5: UNIFIED-AI-REDIRECTS — vendor URLs are forbidden anywhere in the image). Surfaces: `GET /v1/models`, `POST /v1/chat/completions` (SSE streaming + tool calling), `POST /v1/embeddings`. Discovery: `usr/share/mios/ai/v1/models.json`, `usr/share/mios/ai/v1/mcp.json`. Canonical agent prompt: `usr/share/mios/ai/system.md`, override chain `~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md` → `/etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md` → vendor.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
Why does ((COUNT++)) break MiOS phase scripts?

Under `set -euo pipefail`, `((VAR++))` returns the *pre-increment* value as its exit status. When `VAR` was `0`, the expression's value is `0` which bash treats as exit code `1`, killing the script on the very first increment. Use `VAR=$((VAR + 1))` instead. CI fails any script using `((VAR++))` (`ENGINEERING.md` §Shell-conventions).
You are MiOS-Engineer.
Where do I bake a systemd Quadlet?

Vendor-shipped Quadlets go in `usr/share/containers/systemd/`. Host-overridable Quadlets go in `etc/containers/systemd/`. Every Quadlet must declare `User=`, `Group=`, `Delegate=yes` (LAW 6: UNPRIVILEGED-QUADLETS) — the only documented exceptions are `mios-ceph` and `mios-k3s` which are `User=root` because Ceph and K3s require uid 0. Each Quadlet image must also be symlinked into `/usr/lib/bootc/bound-images.d/` (LAW 3: BOUND-IMAGES, binder loop at `automation/08-system-files-overlay.sh:74-86`). Use systemd `Condition*` directives to gate per-host applicability rather than disabling via static config (defaults policy, INDEX.md §5).
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What's the override precedence for the agent system prompt?

Highest to lowest: `~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md` (per-user) → `/etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md` (host/admin) → `/usr/share/mios/ai/system.md` (vendor canonical). The agent loads vendor first, then merges host, then user. `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` at the repo root are per-tool stubs that just point at this chain.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I write to /var during the build?

You don't (LAW 2: NO-MKDIR-IN-VAR). Every `/var/` path must be declared in `usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/*.conf` and created at boot by `systemd-tmpfiles`. The Containerfile explicitly purges `/var/*` (except `tmp` and `cache`) at the end of its main RUN. For user homes, write dotfiles to `/etc/skel/` and let `systemd-sysusers` populate `/var/home/<user>/` at first boot.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What kernel packages may I install in MiOS?

Only `kernel-modules-extra`, `kernel-devel`, `kernel-headers`, `kernel-tools`. Never `kernel` or `kernel-core` — the base image (`ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia`) owns the kernel (LTS Linux 6.12). Upgrading them in-container desyncs the running ABI from the akmod-built NVIDIA modules. `automation/01-repos.sh:65,68` excludes them explicitly.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I disable a feature without changing the image?

Set the boolean to `false` in `~/.config/mios/profile.toml` (or `/etc/mios/profile.toml` for host-wide). Every feature flag ships `true` (defaults policy, INDEX.md §5); the system never disables a component via static config — it relies on systemd `Condition*` directives (e.g. `mios-ceph` needs `/etc/ceph/ceph.conf`+`!container`, `mios-k3s` needs `!wsl,!container`) to short-circuit incompatible components silently.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
Why does the iso recipe in Justfile only mount iso.toml?

Mounting both `bib.toml` and `iso.toml` causes BIB to crash with `found config.json and also config.toml`. The `iso` recipe is a v0.2.0 fix that mounts only `config/artifacts/iso.toml` (which already includes the minsize and other ISO-specific settings). If you author a new BIB type, mount exactly one config TOML.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What does --squash-all do, and why is it forbidden in MiOS?

`podman build --squash-all` collapses every layer into one. It strips the `ostree.final-diffid` OCI metadata that bootc needs, AND it defeats Day-2 delta optimization — every `bootc upgrade` would re-pull the entire image instead of just the changed layers. `ENGINEERING.md` §Containerfile-conventions forbids it. Combined with `bootc-base-imagectl rechunk --max-layers 67` from the `hhd-dev/rechunk` project, properly-layered MiOS images give 5–10× smaller upgrade deltas.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How does MiOS detect which GPU is available?

`automation/34-gpu-detect.sh` runs at first boot and writes `/run/mios/gpu-passthrough.status`. The same flow generates a CDI spec at `/var/run/cdi/nvidia.yaml` via `nvidia-ctk cdi generate` for NVIDIA hosts. Admin overrides go in `/etc/cdi/`. The directory existence is guaranteed by `usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/mios-gpu.conf` on every boot (LAW 2).
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What's lockdown=integrity vs lockdown=confidentiality?

Both are kernel lockdown modes. `integrity` (MiOS choice) prevents anything that could allow modifying or bypassing the running kernel — but allows kexec for in-image testing flows. `confidentiality` (SecureBlue's recommendation) goes further and prevents reading kernel memory, which would block kexec, kgdb, and some debugging. MiOS chose `integrity` to keep the self-build path (Mode 4 — a running MiOS builds the next MiOS) workable. Source: `usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/00-mios.toml`, `SECURITY.md` §Kernel-boot-parameters.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I run the KB against my local Ollama?

Set `MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:11434/v1` (Ollama's OpenAI-compatible surface) and `MIOS_AI_MODEL=qwen2.5:32b` (or any pulled tool-calling model — Llama 3.1+, Qwen 2.5+, Hermes 3+, Firefunction). Then run `python3 var/lib/mios/embeddings/ingest_local.py` to embed `chunks.jsonl` into Qdrant, and use the `srv/mios/api/chat.local.example.json` payload via `curl $MIOS_AI_ENDPOINT/chat/completions ...`. The KB's tool schemas in `usr/lib/mios/tools/chat-completions-api/` are the universal form — drop them into the `tools` array unchanged.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What's the difference between bootc upgrade and bootc switch?

`bootc upgrade` pulls a newer revision of the *currently configured* image reference. `bootc switch <ref>` changes the configured reference itself, then pulls. Both stage a new deployment; reboot to activate, or pass `--apply` to reboot immediately. To roll back: `bootc rollback`. Status: `bootc status [--format=json]`.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
What is rechunk and when do I run it?

`bootc-base-imagectl rechunk --max-layers 67` (from `hhd-dev/rechunk`) re-organizes an OCI image's layer structure into 67 deterministic groups. The result: when one component changes, only that layer is re-pulled by `bootc upgrade` — 5–10× smaller deltas typical. Run it on tag pushes (release builds), not on PR/dev builds. `Justfile:rechunk` invokes it; `mios-build-local.ps1` Phase 3 too.
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I troubleshoot a failing bootc upgrade?

## Diagnosis

```sh
bootc status --format=json | jq .
sudo journalctl -u bootc-fetch-apply-updates -b --no-pager
df -h /sysroot   # bootc 1.5+ pre-flight free-space check
```

## Fix

- If `/sysroot` is full: `sudo bootc rollback` to clear staged, or `sudo ostree admin undeploy <index>` to drop a pinned old deployment.
- If lint failed: rebuild with the offending file fixed (kargs.d format, /var write, etc.).
- If signature failed: re-pull and `cosign verify` against the GitHub Actions OIDC issuer.

## Verify

```sh
sudo bootc upgrade --apply && bootc status --format=json | jq .status.staged
```

Expected: `null` (staged became booted).
You are MiOS-Engineer.
How do I add a custom SELinux module?

Drop a `.te` policy file in `usr/share/selinux/packages/mios/`. The build won't auto-load it (`automation/19-k3s-selinux.sh:46-51` compiles `.te` to `.pp` and stages but doesn't `semodule -i`). Add a `semodule -i /usr/share/selinux/packages/mios/<your_module>.pp` invocation to `automation/37-selinux.sh`, plus any `semanage` calls for booleans/fcontexts the module needs. Existing MiOS modules: `mios_portabled`, `mios_kvmfr`, `mios_cdi`, `mios_quadlet`, `mios_sysext`.
